summary: Jeff Atwood announces an update to his craigslist.org all-city search tool, adding for sale searching capabilities alongside the existing jobs search. The post lists several incremental improvements including subcategory selection, relative date display, visual highlighting for post age, and Google Maps integration. Atwood humorously notes he built this feature based on a single user's request, framing it as 'popular demand.' He closes with a joke about explicitly refusing to add personals search functionality.
key_points: Added for sale item searching to complement existing jobs search across all craigslist cities
Implemented UX improvements like relative dates, age-based highlighting, and Google Maps location integration
Built features based on individual user feedback, showing responsiveness to community requests
Drew a clear line at personals search, injecting humor into feature scope decisions
money_quotes: Due to popular demand one person's request, I added for sale searching
I'm open to any additional feature requests.
Except for an all-cities personals search, because that would be downright freaky. I just don't even want to know.
key_insight: Sometimes one person's feature request is worth implementing, but knowing where to draw the line on scope is equally important.
